Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — Architecture Overview

System Components:

1. Data Ingestion Layer
   Accepts source data via REST API or SFTP. Handles CSV, JSON, and XML formats.
   Supports batch and streaming modes. Built-in format validation and schema enforcement.

2. Transformation Engine
   Applies client-defined mapping rules. Built on Apache Spark.
   Processes up to 500k records/minute. Configurable via YAML rule files.

3. API Gateway
   RESTful interface for downstream consumers. Auth via OAuth 2.0.
   Rate-limited at 1,000 req/min per client. Version-pinned endpoints (v1 stable).

4. Client Dashboard
   React-based web interface. Real-time pipeline monitoring and alerting.
   Role-based access control (admin / analyst / read-only).

5. Data Store
   PostgreSQL (operational data) + S3-compatible object storage (raw file archive).
   Multi-tenant isolation via schema-level separation.

Integration Points:
- Meridian ERP system (inbound data via REST)
- Meridian reporting platform (outbound via webhook)
- NovaBridge internal monitoring stack

Key Design Decisions:
- Stateless processing nodes for horizontal scalability
- End-to-end encryption: TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 at rest
- All PII handled under GDPR-compliant data minimization policy

Project Helios — REST API Reference (Internal)
Last updated: 2026-07-05 | Base URL: https://api.helios.novabridge.internal/v1
Authentication: Bearer token (OAuth 2.0) — Header: Authorization: Bearer <token>

Endpoint                        Method  Description                              Status
------------------------------  ------  ---------------------------------------  -------
/health                         GET     System health check                      Stable
/ingest/batch                   POST    Submit batch data file (CSV/JSON/XML)    Stable
/ingest/stream                  POST    Open streaming ingestion session         Stable
/ingest/status/{job_id}         GET     Check ingestion job status               Stable
/pipeline/runs                  GET     List recent pipeline executions           Stable
/pipeline/runs/{run_id}         GET     Pipeline run details and metrics          Stable
/transform/preview              POST    Preview transformation output (dry-run)   Stable
/dashboard/summary              GET     Aggregate pipeline metrics for dashboard  Stable
/dashboard/alerts               GET     Active pipeline alerts                    Stable
/ingest/jobs/{job_id}           DELETE  Cancel in-progress ingestion job          Stable
/config/mappings                GET     List active transformation mapping rules  Beta
/config/mappings/{id}           PUT     Update a mapping rule                     Beta

Rate limits: 1,000 req/min per authenticated client. HTTP 429 returned when exceeded.
Support contact: api-support@novabridge.com

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)

   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)

   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)

   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

content.text: I've compiled the readiness findings — technical blockers and external dependencies. I'll save the assessment now.

input.content:
Helios Demo Readiness Assessment for Meridian Group — As of July 11, 2026
Demo Date: July 25, 2026 (2 weeks)

Overall Assessment: NOT YET READY, but achievable with focused risk mitigation.

Project Helios is currently listed as ON TRACK with minor risks, with the API gateway (100% complete) and stable core pipelines (auth, CSV/JSON/XML ingestion for 10k records) passing QA. However, integration testing is only 40% complete, dashboard v1 is at 60% (target July 22), and the performance benchmarking report is already overdue. Against Meridian's explicit requirement that the demo include live data ingestion with real data (per meeting notes July 8 and Q3 Roadmap Section 9.3), we currently cannot meet the demo acceptance criteria end-to-end.

Two HIGH severity technical blockers remain open in Sprint 14. HEL-D-014: date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY format, which affects ~40% of Meridian source data. This is task HEL-243, owned by Amir Hassan, and is blocked pending Meridian's clean sample dataset due July 15. HEL-D-015: large file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow and crashes at ~210k records, well below our 500k records/min design target. This undermines the live ingestion narrative. In addition, dashboard validation is incomplete — chart rendering is IN PROGRESS/NOT RUN (TC-602/603) and the critical load-time benchmark for 100k records (TC-604 / HEL-247) has not started, which was already flagged as a concern by Meridian in the last sync.

Three external and operational dependencies must also be resolved before July 25. First, GeoSync v2.1 mapping library license renewal (Risk R-02) is pending legal review due July 14; failure would block mapping functionality in the demo. Second, Meridian IT deliverables — infrastructure diagram due July 12 and UAT environment access — are blocking UAT provisioning (HEL-246 At Risk, overdue since July 1). Third, Q2 deliverables show integration test suite setup rescheduled to July 20, leaving only 5 days before demo sign-off on July 25.

Recommendation: Retain July 25 date but implement mitigation now. 1) Prioritize HEL-D-014 and HEL-D-015 with daily check-ins; create fallback ingestion file (<150k records, ISO 8601 only) for demo contingency if HEL-D-015 cannot be fully fixed. 2) Escalate GeoSync renewal and assign backup to Raj Patel/HEL-248 to close by July 14. 3) Start HEL-247 dashboard benchmark immediately — Derek Okafor is currently the key-person bottleneck (R-05). 4) Confirm receipt of Meridian's sample data on July 15 and agenda delivery by July 18 (Sarah Chen). Without these, we risk showing mock data, violating sponsor expectations and triggering Q3 launch slippage risk noted in the roadmap.

content.text:
Assessment saved as **demo_readiness_assessment.txt**.

**Summary:**
- Helios is **not yet ready** for the July 25 Meridian demo, but can be with mitigation.
- Core blockers: HIGH severity defects HEL-D-014 (MM/DD/YYYY date normalization - affects 40% of Meridian data) and HEL-D-015 (memory overflow >200k records), incomplete dashboard tests (TC-602 to 604), and overdue performance benchmarks.
- External dependencies: GeoSync v2.1 license renewal due July 14, Meridian sample data due July 15, and IT diagram / UAT access.
- Recommendation: Fast-track defect fixes, start dashboard benchmark HEL-247 immediately, close license renewal, and prepare a fallback demo dataset (<150k, ISO 8601 only) in case large-file fix slips, to meet Meridian's live-data requirement.
